atomic_propositions.speaker: patient
atomic_propositions.proposition_text: The patient reports right ankle pain following a fall.

atomic_propositions.speaker: patient
atomic_propositions.proposition_text: The patient describes the right ankle pain as a constant, throbbing pain with warmth.

atomic_propositions.speaker: patient
atomic_propositions.proposition_text: The patient reports icing and elevating the ankle last night.

atomic_propositions.speaker: patient
atomic_propositions.proposition_text: The patient reports taking ibuprofen.

atomic_propositions.speaker: patient
atomic_propositions.proposition_text: The patient states he has previously sprained this ankle.

atomic_propositions.speaker: patient
atomic_propositions.proposition_text: The patient denies numbness or tingling.

atomic_propositions.speaker: clinician
atomic_propositions.proposition_text: The patient's blood pressure was 120/80 mmHg.

atomic_propositions.speaker: clinician
atomic_propositions.proposition_text: The patient's heart rate was 80 bpm.

atomic_propositions.speaker: clinician
atomic_propositions.proposition_text: The patient's SpO2 was 98%.

atomic_propositions.speaker: clinician
atomic_propositions.proposition_text: Physical exam reveals tenderness to palpation of the anterior and lateral ankle.

atomic_propositions.speaker: clinician
atomic_propositions.proposition_text: Capillary refill is less than three seconds at the dorsalis pedis pulse.

atomic_propositions.speaker: clinician
atomic_propositions.proposition_text: The x-ray showed no fractures or bony abnormalities.

atomic_propositions.speaker: clinician
atomic_propositions.proposition_text: The ankle appears swollen.

atomic_propositions.speaker: clinician
atomic_propositions.proposition_text: The clinician prescribes ibuprofen as needed for pain.

atomic_propositions.speaker: clinician
atomic_propositions.proposition_text: The clinician plans to keep the leg elevated when seated.

atomic_propositions.speaker: clinician
atomic_propositions.proposition_text: The clinician plans to ice the ankle five times a day for twenty minutes.

atomic_propositions.speaker: clinician
atomic_propositions.proposition_text: The clinician plans to use an air cast to stabilize the ankle.

atomic_propositions.speaker: clinician
atomic_propositions.proposition_text: The clinician plans to avoid weight-bearing on the leg for one to two days.

atomic_propositions.speaker: clinician
atomic_propositions.proposition_text: The clinician plans to start walking as tolerated after one to two days.

atomic_propositions.speaker: clinician
atomic_propositions.proposition_text: The clinician prescribes to continue ibuprofen as needed for pain.

atomic_propositions.speaker: clinician
atomic_propositions.proposition_text: The clinician plans to follow up in one to two weeks if symptoms persist or if there is no improvement.

atomic_propositions.speaker: clinician
atomic_propositions.proposition_text: The clinician plans to contact the office for possible MRI evaluation if pain continues after one week.
